title: ophrys apifera subsp. trollii
intro: Ophrys apifera subsp. trollii, also known as the honey bee orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Ophrys. It is a type of orchid that is native to certain regions, and its name is derived from its resemblance to a female bee, with a yellow and black striped labellum. The honey bee orchid is used in traditional medicine for its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Ophrys apifera subsp. trollii often include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health issues and support overall well-being.
